chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: رِوَايَةِ حَرِيزٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالْقُبَّةِ عَلَى اَلنِّسَاءِ وَ اَلصِّبْيَانِ وَ هُمْ مُحْرِمُونَ وَ لاَ يَرْتَمِسُ اَلْمُحْرِمُ فِي اَلْمَاءِ وَ لاَ اَلصَّائِمُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.2678 - In the narration of Hariz, Abu Abdullah (as) said: "There is no harm in a tent being set up over women and children while they are in the state of ihram. However, the person in ihram must not immerse themselves in water, nor should someone who is fasting."
